degraded.condition: suite scope (top-level script + its shared test)
degraded.detail: The diff touches scripts/update-marketplace.sh + shared/tests/test_installer_shell_scripts.py only (no plugin code). F0 ran the full shared/tests suite (3741 passed, 16 skipped) + ruff@0.15.15 + bash -n syntax check + leak-guard f0 ALLOW. Plugin suites are unaffected; CI runs the full matrix.

shellcheck is not installed on this machine, so static shell-lint was limited to `bash -n` (syntax). Compensated by the real end-to-end run of the modified script under `set -euo pipefail` (exit 0) which exercised the new install-if-missing branch.

surface_verification.justification: The change is to a top-level developer/install bash script (scripts/update-marketplace.sh) + its shared regression test. No web/UI/API/server surface. The script IS the executable surface and was driven END-TO-END: uninstalled shipwright-grade (recreating the not-installed state), ran the MODIFIED worktree script under set -euo pipefail, and observed it auto-install grade from the marketplace and sync it ('[..] shipwright-grade: not installed - installing from marketplace...' -> '1 installed, 14 synced, 0 skipped, 0 errors'), with grade back in installed_plugins.json @0.29.1 and its cache dir present. Verification = F0 shared/tests (3741) + ruff + bash -n + leak-guard f0 ALLOW + the real-script E2E.

behaviors.behavior: A registered-but-not-installed plugin is installed from the marketplace, not silently skipped
behaviors.evidence: test_update_marketplace_autoinstall.py::test_installs_missing_registered_plugin_instead_of_skipping (static) + E2E: uninstall grade -> run script -> grade re-installed @0.29.1 ('1 installed')

behaviors.behavior: An already-installed plugin is only re-queried + synced, never re-installed (guarded on empty cache path)
behaviors.evidence: E2E: the other 13 plugins synced with 0 re-installs ('1 installed, 14 synced'); the install branch runs only when _install_path returns empty

behaviors.behavior: F37 parity: the new _install_path helper uses $PYTHON_BIN, never a bare `python -c`
behaviors.evidence: test_update_marketplace_autoinstall.py::test_new_helper_keeps_f37_no_bare_python asserts no `$(python -c`; test_installer_shell_scripts.py::test_f37 re-reads the whole src; both pass

behaviors.behavior: The install is non-fatal under set -e (offline/headless run just skips, script does not abort)
behaviors.evidence: the install is inside an `if ...; then` (set -e ignores if-condition failures); the full E2E ran the script under `set -euo pipefail` to exit 0
